calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-31-2022 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John E. Fasesky, via ZOOM, for Janeen L. Schueler, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

The court points counsel to that the will has only witness signatures and no printed names under said signatures. Mr. Fasesky offers oral testimony and points the court to probate code 6111 which asserts that the will submitted with the petition should be admitted as holographic. Mr. Fasesky provides additional oral testimony in support of his petition. The court then turns to the issue of whether a minimum bond of $20,000 will be sufficient. Mr. Fasesky confirms that a minimum bond will be sufficient. The court agrees with counsel. After additional discussion, the court confirms that no one is present to object to this petition. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Dec-01-2001 is admitted to probate. Janeen L. Schueler is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Oct-31-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Continued To Oct-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
